title: Chatbots — Rule-Based to Neural to LLM Agents

questions.question: What does a slot-filling state machine do in a rule-based chatbot?
options: Tracks which required parameters (date, destination, amount) are still missing and asks for them in sequence
questions.explanation: Slot filling iteratively collects the structured parameters a task handler needs.

questions.question: Why is retrieval-based chat resistant to hallucination?
options: It returns a canned response from a curated set rather than generating new text
questions.explanation: Retrieval surfaces pre-written answers; no generation means no fabricated content.

questions.question: What defines an LLM agent loop versus a single-shot LLM call?
options: A controller that interleaves LLM calls with tool invocations until the model returns a final answer or the step budget is hit
questions.explanation: Agents add a plan-act-observe loop with tool calls and a termination condition.

questions.question: Why is hybrid routing (rules + retrieval + LLM agent) the 2026 production default?
options: It removes the need for evaluation
No single architecture handles every request well; rules cover destructive actions, retrieval covers FAQ, agents handle ambiguous open-ended queries
questions.explanation: Hybrid systems use deterministic rules for risky actions and reserve LLM agents for open-ended queries.

questions.question: What is prompt injection?
options: User-supplied (direct) or document-supplied (indirect) text that tries to override the system prompt or hijack the agent's behavior
questions.explanation: Prompt injection rewrites the agent's behavior via untrusted text in user input or tool outputs.

questions.question: Which OWASP Top 10 (LLM Apps 2025) risk is ranked LLM01?
questions.explanation: Prompt injection is LLM01 in the OWASP LLM Apps Top 10 (2025).

questions.question: What mitigation pattern reduces indirect prompt injection by separating planning from execution?
options: Plan-Verify-Execute: the agent plans first, verifies each action against the plan, then executes — preventing tool outputs from injecting new unplanned actions
questions.explanation: PVE checks each step against the agreed plan, so injected instructions from tool outputs are rejected.

questions.question: Why must destructive actions (payments, deletions) route through a structured flow even in an LLM-agent system?
options: Confident fabrication, prompt injection, and scope creep mean the LLM cannot be the sole authority for irreversible side effects
questions.explanation: Hallucination and injection make irreversible actions through pure LLM agents unsafe; require deterministic confirmation flows.
